cards.text: In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.
cards.connection: Provides the foundational worldview that history unfolds under divine sovereignty, not random chaos.
cards.cueContext: Used when establishing the biblical foundation of history.

cards.text: The sons of Ham: Cush, Mizraim, Phut, and Canaan.
cards.connection: Identifies the founders of African civilizations according to the Table of Nations.
cards.cueContext: Shown during the discussion of the African Founders.

cards.text: Cursed be Canaan; a servant of servants he shall be to his brothers.
cards.connection: Clarifies that the curse was on Canaan, refuting the racist 'Curse of Ham' myth used to justify slavery.
cards.cueContext: Used when debunking the Curse of Ham.

cards.text: Do not be deceived: God is not mocked, for whatever one sows, that will he also reap.
cards.connection: Explains how the great African civilizations flourished through common grace but faced judgment for their idolatry.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the spiritual legacy of Egypt, Cush, and Phut.
